2022-07-01.title: ‘Wearing a Piece of History’: Vintage Clothing Stores Find a Growing Market
2022-07-01.abstract: The demand for repurposed clothes is rising in Australia, store owners say, as shoppers look for sustainability and history over fast fashion.

2022-07-01.title: Missile Strikes on Ukraine Kill at Least 21 Near Odesa
2022-07-01.abstract: The strikes hit a residential tower and a recreational center in a coastal town southwest of the Black Sea port city of Odesa.

2022-07-01.title: ‘Long Live the Theater’: Mariupol’s Drama Company to Perform Again
2022-07-01.abstract: Thirteen members of the Mariupol Drama Theater have reconvened in western Ukraine where they are rehearsing a new play about a Ukrainian dissident who died in a Russian prison camp in the 1980s.

2022-07-01.title: North Korea Suggests ‘Alien Things’ From the South Brought Covid
2022-07-01.abstract: Airborne propaganda leaflets sent by activists across the border have long angered the regime, which pointed to them as the likely culprit in the country’s recent outbreak.

2022-07-01.title: Climate Protesters in Australia Face Harsh New Penalties
2022-07-01.abstract: As of April, people in New South Wales who disrupt business activities can face up to two years in prison. Some climate activists say this law is aimed at them.

2022-07-02.title: Ill Prepared for Combat, Volunteers Die in Battles Far From Home
2022-07-02.abstract: Many of the fighters for Ukraine’s western territorial defense units were assigned benign tasks away from the fighting when they first joined. Then they were called to the front to fight.

2022-07-02.title: How the Russian Media Spread False Claims About Ukrainian Nazis
2022-07-02.abstract: An analysis of nearly eight million articles about Ukraine from Russian websites shows that references to Nazism have spiked to unprecedented levels during the war.

2022-07-02.title: Japan’s Secret to Taming the Coronavirus: Peer Pressure
2022-07-02.abstract: The country has never mandated masks or vaccinations, but it’s evaded the worst of Covid, thanks to a fear of public shaming and the “self restraint police.”

2022-07-02.title: Ukraine dreams of rebuilding the world’s largest airplane. Can Richard Branson help?
2022-07-02.abstract: A Virgin Group spokesperson said the effort would turn to finding ways the international community could support the project.

2022-07-02.title: Russia Repeatedly Strikes Ukraine’s Civilians. There’s Always an Excuse.
2022-07-02.abstract: The Kremlin’s explanations have sometimes satisfied the Russian people, but they fall apart under closer scrutiny abroad.

2022-07-03.title: Investigators of War Crimes in Ukraine Face Formidable Challenges
2022-07-03.abstract: International agencies are joining Ukrainian prosecutors in what may be the biggest effort ever to hold war criminals to account. But the difficulties and dangers they face are exceptional, too.

2022-07-03.title: Four killed after explosions in a Russian city near the Ukraine border.
2022-07-03.abstract: The Russians said its air-defense systems shot down three missiles, but one fell to the ground in central Belgorod.

2022-07-03.title: Women who were raped by Russian soldiers yearn for justice.
2022-07-03.abstract: Rapes were among the many atrocities Russian troops inflicted on Ukrainian civilians during weeks of occupation in the Kyiv suburbs and elsewhere. But the challenges of prosecuting the assaults are daunting.

2022-07-03.title: Big Ben’s Bongs Will Soon Ring Out Again Across London
2022-07-03.abstract: Next to the Houses of Parliament, the tower that houses the enormous bell has been undergoing renovation since 2017.

2022-07-03.title: In Northern Ireland, Old Passions Are Smoldering Anew
2022-07-03.abstract: Brexit and so-called legacy legislation are reviving sensitive issues of Catholic and Protestant identity that civic leaders have sought to dampen.

2022-07-04.title: Mall Shooting Shocks Denmark on a Joyful Summer Weekend
2022-07-04.abstract: The suspect in the attack that killed 3 people was known to mental health services, the police said, and the victims appeared to be random.

2022-07-04.title: Nazi Tapes Provide a Chilling Sequel to the Eichmann Trial
2022-07-04.abstract: Sixty years after the execution of Adolf Eichmann, the logistics chief of the Holocaust, an Israeli documentary airs his confessions in his own voice.

2022-07-04.title: Five Years After Vanishing, Chinese Canadian Billionaire Faces Trial
2022-07-04.abstract: The case against the financier Xiao Jianhua is widely seen as part of a crackdown on the debt-fueled excess that drove China’s economic growth.

2022-07-04.title: Macron Adjusts His Cabinet, Seeking a Fresh Start
2022-07-04.abstract: The new appointments by President Emmanuel Macron of France are unlikely to help him push his agenda through a fragmented lower house of Parliament.

2022-07-04.title: Bullet Too Damaged to Prove Who Killed Palestinian American Journalist, U.S. Says
2022-07-04.abstract: The State Department said Shireen Abu Akleh, the Al Jazeera broadcaster killed in the West Bank in May, was likely hit by shots fired from Israeli military positions.

2022-07-05.title: As Young Blood Enters South Korean Politics, New (and Old) Barriers Emerge
2022-07-05.abstract: A new law allowed a record number of young people to run in local elections this year. But they face skepticism, cultural hurdles and problems as old as politics itself.

2022-07-05.title: ‘Get the Stretcher!’ Life and Death on Ukraine’s Front Line
2022-07-05.abstract: On another day of scorched-earth artillery barrages in the east, a team of Ukrainian medics did their best to stabilize and assure the survivors, with the din of war all around.

2022-07-05.title: Ukraine’s prime minister says rebuilding will cost $750 billion.
2022-07-05.abstract: World leaders are mapping out the monumental effort to restore a war-battered nation.

2022-07-05.title: Has Biden’s Top Diplomat in Mexico Gone Too Far, Officials Ask?
2022-07-05.abstract: Administration officials fear that the U.S. ambassador’s cozy relationship with the Mexican president has backfired, and may be setting back American interests in the region.

2022-07-05.title: Britain Gears Up for a Summer of Labor Discontent
2022-07-05.abstract: With energy costs and inflation surging, and taxes and the cost of loans increasing, British workers are demanding higher pay and going on strike with a militancy not seen in years.

2022-07-06.title: Boris Johnson Fights to Hold Power After 2 Top Ministers Jump Ship
2022-07-06.abstract: Two top cabinet members resigned from the scandal-scarred British prime minister’s government. Some wondered if the master of political survival could pull off another save.

2022-07-06.title: How War in Ukraine Roiled Russia’s ‘Coolest Company’
2022-07-06.abstract: The decline of Yandex, the Russian version of Google, is emblematic of the economic and cultural troubles spawned by the invasion.

2022-07-06.title: The decline of Yandex, a Russia tech giant, reflects the domestic troubles stirred by the invasion.
2022-07-06.abstract: What a difference a war makes.

2022-07-06.title: Mass Funeral Deepens Mystery: Why Did 21 South African Teens Die in Tavern?
2022-07-06.abstract: Survivors of the tragedy last month recounted in interviews the presence of a mysterious gas and then a crush to escape. Officials say the stampede didn’t kill them.

2022-07-06.title: What happens next if Johnson quits?
2022-07-06.abstract: If Boris Johnson resigns, here is what could happen next.

2022-07-07.title: A top Russian official threatens the U.S. with the ‘wrath of God’ over war crime investigations.
2022-07-07.abstract: One of Russia’s leading political figures on Wednesday rattled his country’s nuclear capabilities and threatened the United States with the “wrath of God” for pursuing war crimes investigations against Russia.

2022-07-07.title: For Foreign Fighters in Ukraine, a War Unlike Any They’ve Seen
2022-07-07.abstract: Small groups of Western combat veterans are on the front lines, but many more with fewer skills are left trying to find a role to play.

2022-07-07.title: The Pincher scandal is only the latest in a long line of controversies for Johnson.
2022-07-07.abstract: The fallout from Boris Johnson’s appointment of Chris Pincher to a government post, and Downing Street’s shifting account of the circumstances around the decision, dominated the recent political conversation in Britain.

2022-07-07.title: Glacier Tragedy Shows Reach of Europe’s New Heat
2022-07-07.abstract: A collapse at the top of one of Italy’s tallest peaks offered the latest evidence of the deadly threat posed by rising temperatures.

2022-07-07.title: Beijing Introduces a Local Vaccine Pass and Urges Older People to Get Shots
2022-07-07.abstract: The city announced the change months after other countries had put vaccine passports in place.

2022-07-08.title: At Europe’s Largest Port, Russia Sanctions Meet Their Toughest Test
2022-07-08.abstract: The Dutch port of Rotterdam and its staff are at the front line of enforcing the European Union’s restrictions on trade with Russia. It’s no easy task.

2022-07-08.title: Videos Show the Moments After Shinzo Abe Is Shot
2022-07-08.abstract: Footage shared on social media showed the scene after Shinzo Abe, the former prime minister of Japan, was assassinated while giving a speech in western Japan.

2022-07-08.title: For foreign fighters, the war is unlike any they’ve seen.
2022-07-08.abstract: DRUZHKIVKA, Ukraine — Four months after Russia invaded Ukraine, foreign combat veterans who answered the Ukrainian president’s call to fight are grappling with the grueling reality of a war unlike any they have seen.

2022-07-08.title: Divisions over the war play out at a G20 meeting as Russia prepares for an assault on Donetsk in eastern Ukraine.
2022-07-08.abstract: As foreign ministers from the Group of 20 industrialized nations met in Bali on Friday to discuss some of the world’s most pressing challenges, including the war in Ukraine, the divisions playing out as a result of the fighting were to the fore diplomatically, too.

2022-07-08.title: Johnson’s Lies Worked for Years, Until They Didn’t
2022-07-08.abstract: The British prime minister thought he could swagger and dissemble his way through any scandal, but found the rules of gravity applied after all.

2022-07-09.title: Just Months After the Last Floods, It’s the ‘Same Thing Again’
2022-07-09.abstract: The toll of recurring disasters is starting to show among the residents of New South Wales, with many desperate for a break from overflowing rivers and wildfires.

2022-07-09.title: A shocking crime with many unanswered questions.

2022-07-09.title: The assassination shocked a nearly gun-free Japan.
2022-07-09.abstract: The assassination of former Prime Minister Shinzo Abe during a campaign rally in western Japan was especially hard to fathom because it involved a gun — a type of crime that is extremely rare in a country with some of the most stringent laws on buying and owning firearms.

2022-07-09.title: ‘It’s Like Parallel Realities’: Rituals of Life and Death Blur in a Vibrant Ukrainian City
2022-07-09.abstract: In Lviv, babies are born in a hospital just steps away from the military cemetery where Ukraine’s young soldiers are laid to rest.

2022-07-09.title: Questions Arise About Abe’s Security Protection After Assassination
2022-07-09.abstract: Numerous videos show the gunman walking unobstructed past security before pointing a large handmade gun in the direction of the former prime minister.

2022-07-10.title: Desperate for Recruits, Russia Launches a ‘Stealth Mobilization’
2022-07-10.abstract: Leery of a national draft for the Ukraine war, the Kremlin is offering cash bonuses and employing strong arm tactics.

2022-07-10.title: As they took over the president’s house, Sri Lankan protesters stopped to savor its luxuries.
2022-07-10.abstract: Images from Colombo showed demonstrators enjoying a rare moment of levity, after months of economic deprivation.

2022-07-10.title: The president has not been heard from since protesters overran his residence, fueling questions about who is in charge.
2022-07-10.abstract: The uncertainty about who is leading Sri Lanka deepened on Sunday, as protesters occupied the halls of the presidential residence and office and President Gotabaya Rajapaksa had not yet addressed reports that he plans to resign later this week.

2022-07-10.title: Sri Lanka needs billions of dollars as it exhausts its sources of financing.
2022-07-10.abstract: Funds provided by India are already gone. China has kept its distance. As the crisis deepens, Sri Lanka has even turned to charities in Qatar for help.

2022-07-10.title: In the capital of Sri Lanka, high spirits and curiosity from the protesters.
2022-07-10.abstract: Hours after protesters took control the building, it has effectively become a free museum. People stream in and admire the art as Army guards quietly patrol the halls.

2022-07-11.title: Inside the Amazon Journey That Left a Journalist and an Activist Dead
2022-07-11.abstract: Dom Phillips and Bruno Pereira set off deep into the Amazon to meet Indigenous groups patrolling the forest. Then they vanished.

2022-07-11.abstract: The island nation was once an economic hope, with a growing middle class. Now many people are living on the edge, unable to buy even the basics.

2022-07-11.abstract: Thousands of Cubans took to the streets last July protesting economic misery. A year later, hundreds are in jail and the financial crisis has only worsened.

2022-07-11.title: Where Mantras Trump Medicine, Vaccines Are a ‘Violation’
2022-07-11.abstract: The Baduy, an Indigenous group in Indonesia, believe that vaccinations are impure, and unnecessary, and that their beliefs protect them. To skeptics, they note their Covid death toll: zero.

2022-07-11.abstract: The cities of Xi’an, Lanzhou and Haikou set restrictions on several million residents. In Macau, the gambling hub, authorities shut casinos and ordered everyone to stay home.

2022-07-12.title: ‘I Don’t Mind Being a Martyr’: Ailing Hong Kong Activist Defiant in Court
2022-07-12.abstract: Koo Sze-yiu, who has been diagnosed with late-stage colon cancer, was sentenced to nine months in prison for planning a protest, amid fears he could die in prison.

2022-07-12.title: Is the World Really Falling Apart, or Does It Just Feel That Way?
2022-07-12.abstract: By most measures — with one glaring exception — people around the world are better off than ever. So why doesn’t it feel that way, especially to Americans?

2022-07-12.title: Drought Tightens Grip on Northern Italy
2022-07-12.abstract: A prolonged dry spell caused by increasingly dry winters, higher temperatures and less rain has put the fertile region’s rice harvest and other crops at risk.

2022-07-12.title: Tricky diplomatic terrain awaits as Russian leader travels to Tehran.
2022-07-12.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin will visit Tehran next week for meetings with the leaders of Iran and Turkey, taking him into challenging diplomatic terrain as he seeks to burnish ties with two nations sometimes aligned, and sometimes sharply at odds, with Russia and with each other.

2022-07-12.title: In West Africa and Beyond, Mali’s Famed Manuscripts Are Put to Use
2022-07-12.abstract: Tens of thousands of manuscripts were smuggled out of Timbuktu under jihadists’ noses, containing a wealth of knowledge about science, governance and peace-making. Now the public is getting a look.

2022-07-13.title: Why protesters demanded the president’s resignation.
2022-07-13.abstract: For many Sri Lankans, food and fuel became either impossible to find or too expensive to buy.

2022-07-13.abstract: Sri Lanka’s president, Gotabaya Rajapaksa, has fled to the Maldives, whose leaders have close ties with the president and his family.

2022-07-13.abstract: The bats and balls were real, but not much else was authentic in what officials said was an elaborate scheme to dupe Russian gamblers through hoax games streamed on YouTube.

2022-07-13.abstract: The spotlight is now on a parliament that has long frustrated the island nation of 22 million, with lawmakers and political parties engaging in protracted and messy fights over positions of power.

2022-07-13.abstract: Negotiators did not reach a deal to allow grain to flow from blockaded Ukrainian ports and ease a global food crisis, but the U.N. secretary general said they made progress.

2022-07-14.title: The U.S. accuses Russia of war crimes, alleging hundreds of thousands of Ukrainian deportations.

2022-07-14.title: Witnesses testify in Brittney Griner’s defense as her drug trial continues in Russia.

2022-07-14.abstract: President Biden’s visit during an Israeli election campaign may have been unavoidable, given that voters are heading to the polls for a fifth time in under four years.

2022-07-15.title: Macron warns of ‘likely risk’ France will be cut off from Russian natural gas.

2022-07-15.abstract: Beyond logistical issues, the uneven flow of arms to Ukraine hints at differences among allies about whether Russia should be punished or eventually accommodated.

2022-07-15.abstract: Hussein al-Sheikh is considered a possible successor to Mahmoud Abbas, 87, the Palestinian Authority president. But as a liaison to Israel, some critics call him a “spokesman of the occupation.”

2022-07-15.title: Smile or Be Disciplined, Philippine Mayors Warn City Workers
2022-07-15.abstract: “Frowning is not allowed in the municipality!” said the mayor of Mulanay in the Philippines. Many residents applauded his new policy.

2022-07-15.abstract: Russian attacks on civilians have underscored the need to hold war crimes to account. But the prosecutor of the International Criminal Court warned against a “stampede” of investigations.

2022-07-16.title: Putin Aims to Shape a New Generation of Supporters, Through Schools
2022-07-16.abstract: Sweeping changes in the curriculum will soon cast the Russian president as a pivotal historical figure and mark an end of openness to the West.

2022-07-16.title: Egypt’s Revolving Jailhouse Door: One Pretrial Detention After Another
2022-07-16.abstract: An analysis of handwritten records shows that thousands of Egyptians have been detained without charge, sometimes for years.

2022-07-16.title: How We Counted Egypt’s Invisible Detainees
2022-07-16.abstract: There was no public accounting of the prisoners in the black hole of Egypt’s indefinite pretrial detention. Until now.

2022-07-16.title: Dead but Not Buried, Ex-President’s Body Sparks a Cross-Continental Fight
2022-07-16.abstract: José Eduardo dos Santos ruled Angola for 38 years. But he died in Spain. A dispute over where to lay him to rest has divided his family and could decide the fate of his political party.

2022-07-16.title: Passengers Sigh as Heathrow Caps Numbers to Head Off ‘Airmageddon’
2022-07-16.abstract: After two years of pandemic-enforced staycations, demand for travel has come roaring back in Europe, and airports are finding it impossible to keep up.

2022-07-17.title: Prime Minister Race in Britain Remains Unsettled in Wake of Johnson’s Downfall
2022-07-17.abstract: Rishi Sunak, the former chancellor of the Exchequer, is the current front-runner, but Penny Mordaunt, a junior trade minister, is making a surprisingly strong run for the leadership position.

2022-07-17.abstract: When he was alive, the graffiti of Tsang Tsou-choi, or the “King of Kowloon,” was considered peculiar and personal. In a radically changed city, his mostly vanished art now has a political charge.

2022-07-17.title: Extreme Heat Continues Its March Across Western Europe
2022-07-17.abstract: A heat wave that has caused fires to rage in southwestern France and Spain is moving north toward Britain, which may see its highest recorded temperatures ever.

2022-07-17.title: Ukrainian Plane Carrying Serbian Munitions Bound for Bangladesh Crashes in Greece
2022-07-17.abstract: Eight Ukrainian citizens were killed when the cargo plane transporting 11.5 tons of weapons crashed in a field in Greece.

2022-07-17.title: A Young Woman’s Wartime Task: Persuading People to Leave Their Homes
2022-07-17.abstract: In phone calls, doorstep conversations and public speeches, Yana Muravinets has tried to convince people that it’s time to leave villages around the embattled southern city of Mykolaiv.

2022-07-18.title: Extreme Temperatures Feed Wildfires Across Europe
2022-07-18.abstract: A heat wave has caused fires to rage in southwestern France and Spain.

2022-07-18.title: Reunited in Bucha, a Ukrainian Family Comes to Terms With War’s Traumas
2022-07-18.abstract: As flowers bloom amid the wreckage, the Kyiv suburb synonymous with atrocity feels almost normal again. But the lives of the people there may never be.

2022-07-18.title: Mexico’s Leader Says Poverty Is His Priority. But His Policies Hurt the Poor.
2022-07-18.abstract: Halfway into the president’s term, the plight of Mexico’s lower classes has only worsened. Economists say it is the result of government mismanagement of welfare programs and the economy.

2022-07-18.abstract: Prime Minister Fumio Kishida says he will pursue Shinzo Abe’s goals, such as a stronger military. But he has also set himself apart from his slain predecessor.

2022-07-18.title: ‘Please do all you can to bring us home’: Brittney Griner is not the only American who’s been ‘wrongfully detained.’
2022-07-18.abstract: Brittney Griner. Austin Tice. The Citgo 6. And now, potentially, three American military veterans who were captured by enemy forces after traveling to Ukraine to fight Russia.

2022-07-19.title: Russia fines Google for failing to remove news it calls ‘fake.’
2022-07-19.abstract: A Russian court fined Google about $360 million (21.1 billion rubles) on Monday for failing to remove content the country deems illegal, including coverage of the war in Ukraine.

2022-07-19.title: Bolsonaro Gathers Foreign Diplomats to Cast Doubt on Brazil’s Elections
2022-07-19.abstract: President Jair Bolsonaro elevated his vote-fraud claims from a matter of domestic politics to foreign policy, heightening international fears that he would dispute the coming election.

2022-07-19.title: ‘My Worldview Has Been Destroyed’: Chinese Banking Scandal Tests Faith in the System
2022-07-19.abstract: The disappearance of ordinary savers’ money, and the government’s seemingly indifferent response, could pose a major test for the Communist Party’s legitimacy.

2022-07-19.title: Words of War: A Literary Lifeline for the Battlefield
2022-07-19.abstract: Does poetry have any place in a war zone? For one correspondent, it is indispensable.

2022-07-19.title: Outbreaks in China prompt lockdowns and fears of more economic woes.
2022-07-19.abstract: The emergence of more infectious subvariants of the coronavirus has triggered mass quarantines, including 2,000 tourists stranded in a popular beach resort town.

2022-07-20.title: The highest-ranked Russian in women’s tennis calls the war ‘a full-blown nightmare.’
2022-07-20.abstract: For the first time since Russia invaded Ukraine in February, a Russian tennis player has publicly criticized the war, a move that may land her in trouble if and when she returns to her home country.

2022-07-20.title: Sea Turtles Found Stabbed in Japan, but Charges Are Unlikely
2022-07-20.abstract: Despite being endangered, green sea turtles are not protected by the country’s conservation laws. They often get tangled in nets, to the annoyance of fishermen.

2022-07-20.title: Ally of Ousted Sri Lanka President Is Chosen to Replace Him
2022-07-20.abstract: Ranil Wickremesinghe, a canny political survivor with ties to the exiled former president, inherits a nation in deep crisis.

2022-07-20.title: Poetry and artillery: A reading list for war.
2022-07-20.abstract: Even the newest of correspondents knows not to go into a war zone without the right training, the right gear, and the right exit plan. But some seasoned reporters have learned that they need something more to sustain them through the bleak days and nights of carnage: poetry.

2022-07-20.title: Draghi Government Falls Apart, Returning Turbulent Politics to Italy
2022-07-20.abstract: Days after he tendered his resignation, the Italian leader tried one more time to convince the country’s fractious parties to hang together. They refused.

2022-07-21.title: The C.I.A. director says Putin believes the U.S. will forget about Ukraine.
2022-07-21.abstract: The C.I.A. director says that President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia believes that America will suffer from “attention deficit disorder” and lose interest and resolve in the Ukraine war.

2022-07-21.title: Austin Says U.S. Will Send More Military Equipment to Ukraine
2022-07-21.abstract: Defense Secretary Lloyd J. Austin III announced that the U.S. military would send additional advanced rocket systems to help Ukraine defend itself.

2022-07-21.title: A Village Retaken, and a Confidence Boost for Ukraine’s Troops
2022-07-21.abstract: A Ukrainian brigade surprised Russian forces to score a rare success in the east and send a signal to other units that the Russians can be routed in this region, too.

2022-07-21.title: Russia restarts gas shipments through a key pipeline to Germany.
2022-07-21.abstract: The suspense was another reminder of the reliance of Germany, and much of the rest of Europe, on Russian energy. European Union leaders urged members to cut natural gas use immediately.

2022-07-21.title: Russia resumes gas flow through key pipeline to Germany.
2022-07-21.abstract: Gas began flowing early Thursday through a pipeline that normally provides more than a third of Germany’s needs as well as essential gas supplies to several of its European partners.

2022-07-22.title: Sri Lankan Forces Raid Protest Camp at Center of Uprising
2022-07-22.abstract: The predawn raid in Colombo on Friday caught demonstrators by surprise. The police detained at least nine people.

2022-07-22.title: As Covid Cases Rise Again, No Clear Guidance From the Top
2022-07-22.abstract: Omicron subvariants are driving a winter surge in Australia, but officials’ health advice has been confusing, to put it mildly.

2022-07-22.title: 1947: Deadly Volcanic Eruption in Nicaragua Menaces Nearby City
2022-07-22.abstract: Hot rocks and ash were spewed over the countryside, destroying some of the Central American country’s richest farmland and leaving thousands homeless.

2022-07-22.title: ​South Korea’s New President Calls for Criminal Investigation of Past Government
2022-07-22.abstract: Newly released photos of two North Korean men being deported under Moon Jae-in have revived accusations of a potential “crime against humanity.”

2022-07-22.title: After Mocking France’s Literary Elite, a Fraught Invite Into the Club
2022-07-22.abstract: Mohamed Mbougar Sarr, a Senegal-born writer, has won high praise and top prizes from Paris’s insular publishing establishment. But the novelist wonders: Is it an endorsement or “a way to silence me”?

2022-07-23.title: Spouses of world leaders to join Ukraine’s first lady in a discussion on postwar lives.
2022-07-23.abstract: The spouses of world leaders will be talking about the postwar reconstruction of Ukraine this weekend during the second Summit of First Ladies and Gentlemen, hosted by Olena Zelenska, the nation’s first lady.

2022-07-23.title: The State Department says two Americans died recently in Donbas.
2022-07-23.abstract: Two Americans recently died in Ukraine’s eastern Donbas region, the U.S. State Department said on Friday.

2022-07-23.title: In Japan, Abe Suspect’s Grudge Against Unification Church Is a Familiar One
2022-07-23.abstract: The man accused of killing Shinzo Abe said his mother’s huge donations to the church bankrupted his family. Many others in Japan have similar stories.

2022-07-23.title: The Times’s Theater Critic Reviews Stratford’s New Theater
2022-07-23.abstract: The Stratford Festival in Ontario opened a glamorous new theater last month that prioritizes the theater itself, not just what surrounds it.

2022-07-23.title: Missiles strike Odesa, hitting a critical port for global grain supplies.
2022-07-23.abstract: The explosions risk destabilizing an international deal meant to reopen shipping routes seen as critical for easing concerns over world food supplies

2022-07-24.title: Last Stand at Azovstal: Inside the Siege That Shaped the Ukraine War
2022-07-24.abstract: For 80 days, at a sprawling steelworks, a relentless Russian assault met unyielding Ukrainian resistance. This is how it was for those who fought, and for those trapped beneath the battlefield.

2022-07-24.title: Pope to Offer a Long-Sought Apology to Canada’s Indigenous People
2022-07-24.abstract: Pope Francis will travel to an Indigenous community in Canada to apologize for the church’s role in residential schools that tried to erase Indigenous culture, and where thousands of children died.

2022-07-24.title: Fears rise for a rights activist captured while fighting for Ukraine.
2022-07-24.abstract: Russian propaganda has demonized Maksym Butkevych, a former journalist and campaigner who was taken prisoner in the eastern city of Sievierodonetsk.

2022-07-24.title: ‘An abyss of fear’: A report accuses Russia of further abuses against civilians.
2022-07-24.abstract: Human Rights Watch cataloged a litany of torture, inhumane treatment, arbitrary detention and disappearances in southern Ukraine and said that the atrocities could amount to war crimes.

2022-07-24.title: A French University Confronts Medical Crimes and Its Nazi Past
2022-07-24.abstract: A report on the University of Strasbourg’s takeover by Nazis during World War II and on medical crimes they committed there sheds light on a period that had been mostly scrubbed from official memory.

2022-07-25.title: From Flight Attendant to Funeral Planner: New Beginnings in the Covid Era
2022-07-25.abstract: The pandemic upended careers in Hong Kong and around the world, forcing or inspiring people to make radical changes in their lives.

2022-07-25.title: As Ukraine Signs Up Soldiers, Questions Arise About How It Chooses
2022-07-25.abstract: Recruiters approach young men on the street, but the standards are not always clear and there are reports of unwilling men being signed up while some eager to fight are turned away.

2022-07-25.title: Myanmar Executes Four Pro-Democracy Activists, Defying Foreign Leaders
2022-07-25.abstract: They were the country’s first executions in more than 30 years. Daw Aung San Suu Kyi, the ousted civilian leader who was detained during last year’s coup, remains in prison.

2022-07-25.title: Tunisians Vote on Constitution That Could Threaten Their Democracy
2022-07-25.abstract: The new charter would enshrine into law a vast expansion of executive power under President Kais Saied in the past year.

2022-07-25.title: U.K., Eurovision Runner-up to Ukraine, Will Host Song Contest in 2023
2022-07-25.abstract: Organizers had ruled out Ukraine from hosting next year because of safety concerns from Russia’s invasion.

2022-07-26.title: Pope Apologizes in Canada for Schools That Abused Indigenous Children
2022-07-26.abstract: Francis, responding to longtime pleas from Indigenous people, begged forgiveness for schools where children were forced to assimilate, many were sexually or physically abused and some died.

2022-07-26.title: Kyiv Nightlife Comes Back Amid Urge for Contact. ‘This Is the Cure.’
2022-07-26.abstract: Frenzied raves. Crowded bars (with free therapy). And of course, cuddle parties. Nightlife is returning to Ukraine’s capital. But revelers still have to reckon with guilty feelings. Plus curfew.

2022-07-26.title: Hotter, Longer and More Widespread Heat Waves Scorch China
2022-07-26.abstract: More than 70 weather stations across the country have recorded their highest temperatures ever in recent weeks.

2022-07-26.title: Brittney Griner’s Lawyers Argue for Leniency in Russian Court
2022-07-26.abstract: The W.N.B.A. star had not intended to smuggle drugs into Russia, her legal team said, adding that, like many other international athletes, she had used cannabis to help ease pain from injuries.

2022-07-26.title: Navigating mines and threatened by war, ships laden with grain are expected to leave Odesa soon.
2022-07-26.abstract: Such voyages would be the first since the signing of a deal to allow a resumption of Ukraine’s grain exports, which have been blocked since Russia’s invasion five months ago.

2022-07-27.title: Strong Quake Hits Northern Philippines, Killing at Least 4
2022-07-27.abstract: The 7.0 magnitude quake on Luzon island damaged buildings and caused dozens of landslides, officials said. At least 44 people were hurt.

2022-07-27.title: Looming Question for Putin Opponents: Can You Change Russia From Jail?
2022-07-27.abstract: For opposition figures choosing to stay in Russia after President Vladimir V. Putin’s invasion of Ukraine, imprisonment looks like a matter of time. It’s also a subject for fierce debate.

2022-07-27.title: Nightlife is returning to Ukraine’s capital. But revelers have to reckon with guilt, and a curfew.
2022-07-27.abstract: KYIV, Ukraine — The rave had been planned for weeks, with the space secured and the D.J.s, the drinks, the invites and the security all lined up.

2022-07-27.title: For Hong Kong’s Beijing-Backed Officials, Xi’s All That
2022-07-27.abstract: The city’s leaders are rushing to embrace Xi Jinping, China’s leader, a performance of devotion that is a jarring shift for Hong Kong’s once rambunctious political culture.

2022-07-27.title: Germany Counts on Chilled Gas to Keep Warm Over Winter
2022-07-27.abstract: As Russia squeezes the flow of natural gas, German officials are urgently turning toward an option they had earlier disregarded: liquefied natural gas.

2022-07-28.title: With Beijing and Washington at odds over Russia, China’s domestic issues hang over a Biden-Xi call.
2022-07-28.abstract: When President Biden gets on a call with President Xi Jinping of China on Thursday to try to tamp down tension over Taiwan, the two will have a laundry list of mutual grievances to address. But one source of recent friction may be something not on the list: China’s domestic troubles.

2022-07-28.title: Britain’s Power Grid Warns of a Tight Energy Supply This Winter
2022-07-28.abstract: Unlike many European countries, Britain produces substantial amounts of its own gas, but it has still faced skyrocketing energy prices in recent months.

2022-07-28.title: The Hunt Is On for ‘War Trophies’ in Ukraine
2022-07-28.abstract: Collectors across the country are seeking pieces of shrapnel, bits of bombs and even the uniforms of dead Russians. It’s part of an urge to feel more directly connected to the cause.

2022-07-28.title: The U.S. Senate passes a resolution seeking to label Russia as a sponsor of terrorism.
2022-07-28.abstract: The Senate unanimously approved a nonbinding resolution on Wednesday calling for Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken to designate Russia as a state sponsor of terrorism for actions in Chechnya, Georgia, Syria and Ukraine that resulted “in the deaths of countless innocent men, women and children.”

2022-07-28.title: Domestic opposition to Putin invites jail, dividing activists over the wisdom of staying put to fight.
2022-07-28.abstract: Shortly after Russia shocked the world by attacking Ukraine on Feb. 24, Ilya V. Yashin, a local Moscow councilman and prominent opposition figure, decided it was time to see a dentist.

2022-07-29.title: Dry Fountains, Cold Pools, Less Beer? Germans Tiptoe Up the Path to Energy Savings
2022-07-29.abstract: Local leaders find themselves at the front line of Europe’s conservation efforts, fearing a Russian gas cut. It’s not an easy place to be.

2022-07-29.title: Large Video Screen Falls During Mirror Concert in Hong Kong, Injuring 2
2022-07-29.abstract: Two dancers for the popular boy band were hospitalized, a local news outlet reported. Hong Kong officials promised an investigation.

2022-07-29.title: With Russia using energy as leverage, the quest in many parts of Europe is to shrink demand.
2022-07-29.abstract: Germany may be most vulnerable to Russia’s energy squeeze, but many other countries are also facing, at minimum, high prices and restricted supples.

2022-07-29.title: I’ll Trade You Some Cursed Baby Dolls and Metal Frogs for That Unwanted Banana Statue
2022-07-29.abstract: John Oliver is willing to make a deal for a piece of (formerly) public art in Fitzroy. That doesn’t mean negotiations have been easy.

2022-07-29.title: Dr. Oh, ‘the God of Parenting,’ Will See You Now. On Television.
2022-07-29.abstract: In South Korea, Dr. Oh Eun-young, a celebrated psychiatrist, has helped destigmatize seeking therapy and blown up the traditionally private relationship with patients by putting it all on TV.

2022-07-30.title: Vessels await word that they can carry the first grain shipments out of Ukraine.
2022-07-30.abstract: ODESA, Ukraine — The first shipments of grain since the beginning of the war in Ukraine have been loaded onto freighters at Ukrainian ports on the Black Sea, getting ready for their first voyages in more than five months.

2022-07-30.title: Heavy Rain Causes Deadly Flooding Across Iran
2022-07-30.abstract: The monsoon-like rains, unusual for the typical dry season in the region, have killed more than 50 people, caused heavy damage and shut down major roadways.

2022-07-30.title: Gangs Advance on the Seat of Haitian Government Power: ‘Haitians Are Hostages’
2022-07-30.abstract: Over 470 people have been killed, injured or are missing in recent violence, according to the U.N., as gangs expand their control and threaten the presidential palace.

2022-07-30.title: A Polish Priest’s War Against Abortion Focuses on Helping Single Mothers
2022-07-30.abstract: The Rev. Tomasz Kancelarczyk sees little effect on women’s decisions whether to have a child from a 29-year abortion ban. What may matter more is support for women who choose to have a child.

2022-07-30.title: A European official denounces ‘inhumane, barbaric acts’ as dozens of prisoners of war are killed.
2022-07-30.abstract: As international outrage swelled, Ukraine on Saturday called on global organizations including the United Nations and the International Committee of the Red Cross to quickly investigate an explosion that ripped through a Russian prison camp holding dozens of Ukrainian soldiers, leaving only charred bodies and twisted metal bunks.

2022-07-31.title: As Latin America Shifts Left, Leaders Face a Short Honeymoon
2022-07-31.abstract: All six of the region’s largest economies could soon be run by presidents elected on leftist platforms. Their challenge? Inflation, war in Europe and growing poverty at home.

2022-07-31.title: Pelosi Heads for Singapore, but Is Silent on Taiwan
2022-07-31.abstract: Speculation about a stop in Taiwan, where she would be the highest-ranking U.S. visitor in decades, has jangled nerves in both Beijing and Washington.

2022-07-31.title: A Nigerian Street Vendor Is Beaten to Death in Italy as Witnesses Stand By
2022-07-31.abstract: The killing, in a seaside town on the Adriatic, has shocked Italians because of its brutality and because of the indifference of those looking on.

2022-07-31.title: One of Ukraine’s richest businessmen is killed in the port city of Mykolaiv.
2022-07-31.abstract: Oleksiy Vadaturskyi, whose company built storage facilities and infrastructure necessary for exporting grain, was killed with his wife in a missile strike.

2022-07-31.title: In Libyan Town Searching for Justice, a Struggle Even to Find Graves
2022-07-31.abstract: Libya’s political chaos has left little room for the reconciliation that many residents see as necessary for peace. Few places feel that more than Tarhuna, where a murderous band of brothers reigned for years.
